description: Logging or error-tracing a credential value copies it into log stores where it is exposed to anyone with log access.
body: Failure mode: a secret held in a variable is printed by a debug log, embedded in an exception message, or emitted in CI output, so it lands in persistent logs. Detection signals: a credential value appears in application logs, stack traces, or build output; log readers exceed the secret's intended audience; a scan of logs flags it. Do not log secret values or pass them into error messages. Safer fallback: redact secrets at the logging boundary, keep them out of exception text and build output, rotate any value that reached shared logs, and escalate the exposure.
